claim: CD28 signals regulate trafficking of murine auto-reactive T cells into target tissues.
evidence_sentences: Here, we demonstrate that the CD28 co-stimulatory pathway regulates the trafficking of self-reactive Ctla4(-/-) T cells to tissues.

Concurrent ablation of the CD28-activated Tec family kinase ITK does not block spontaneous T cell activation but instead causes self-reactive Ctla4(-/-) T cells to accumulate in secondary lymphoid organs.

conditions: trafficking to target tissues vs accumulation in secondary lymphoid organs
